Name: Cloud-executor pilot: round-trip 2-3 coverage WIs through claude.ai/code (time-boxed spike)
Description: Operator-ruled pilot (2026-07-04): evaluate whether bounded executor work items can round-trip through claude.ai/code cloud sessions with estate lifecycle discipline intact. EXECUTION CONSTRAINT (operator ruling): driven FROM RAMTOP (the PM host) — cloud sessions are launched from the Ramtop side, not orion; the Coverage Debt lane only supplies the guinea-pig items. Scope: pick 2-3 code-only items from WI-1401..1414 (no device, no secrets-sensitive surface); dispatch each to a claude.ai/code session; PR back; run normal review. Deliverable is a FINDINGS NOTE, not the code: lifecycle fit (claim/complete discipline from a cloud session), secrets boundaries (what the cloud session could and could not access, and what it SHOULD not), commit-scope and hook behavior, throughput/latency, and a go/no-go recommendation for standing cloud capacity (the VM question). Time-box: 1-2 days elapsed, not effort.

Name: Mobile cross-cutting seam coverage gaps (celebration dedup per-profile, FeedbackProvider, profile-scope drift, idempotency)
Description: WHY: Four cross-cutting invariants that sit on nav-shell/profile-swap seams lack coverage: mobile celebration dedup is instance-scoped not per-profile, FeedbackProvider has zero tests, there is no profile-scoped-keys drift guard, and the session-completed idempotency handler is never double-invoked. RISK: a profile switch could replay celebrations or leak cross-profile cache; a duplicate session-close could double-fire. START: add a profile-switch celebration-dedup test, a FeedbackProvider shake->sheet test, an allow-list<->query-key drift guard, and a double-invoke idempotency test.

Name: S3 avatar-admin parity coverage — sheet omits + doesn't test rare rows before S6 More-tab deletion
Description: WHY: The flow map's S6 gate requires the S3 AccountAdminSheet to cite every rehomed account row before the More tab is deleted; it cites only 5 rows + sign-out and does not even render devices/withdrawal-archive/breakdown-sharing. RISK: More-tab deletion at S6 would silently drop behavior that is only tested in the legacy screens, not at the V2 consolidation point. START: rule WI-1416/D4 first (must the sheet SURFACE the 3 rows, or is transitive reach OK) — that decides whether this is build+test or test-only; then add the missing sheet-row + rare-row-e2e coverage.

Name: Dictation/Homework test coverage gaps (TTS-reset regression, dictation API hooks, attach-failure hook, gallery e2e)
Description: WHY: Dictation/Homework happy paths are covered, but a named TTS-failure-reset bugfix has no regression test, three dictation API hooks are untested, the homework attach-failure hook (use-image-base64) has no test file at all, and the gallery entry-path has no e2e. RISK: named bug-fixes silently regress; the attach-failure/timeout surface is entirely unguarded; an explicit scope item (gallery, 'not just camera OCR') is untested. START: add the TTS-reset regression test, hook tests for the 3 dictation hooks + use-image-base64, and a gallery homework e2e.

Name: Practice/Quiz test coverage gaps (recitation mode, GuessWho skip/wrong, dispute payload, discovery + save-and-finish e2e)
Description: WHY: Practice/Quiz picker/launch are well tested, but recitation session mode has no test at all and several named play/history behaviors are asserted nowhere (GuessWho wrong/skip, dispute payload, vocab language suffix). RISK: a whole session mode and named quiz behaviors can break undetected; two e2e paths (home discovery card, Save&Finish) are also uncovered. START: add co-located tests for the 4 named jest gaps and seed the 2 e2e paths; also fix the flow-inventory:259 QUIZ-14 'None' note (it IS covered).

Name: Library/progress write-gating + data-isolation test coverage gaps
Description: WHY: Library/progress read paths are strong but the write side is under-tested and client gates are inconsistent (vocabulary delete has no client gate at all), and progress data-isolation gates are untested. RISK: a proxy viewer sees delete/write affordances the server rejects (bad UX + no regression guard); a profileId-honor or isViewingSelf privacy gate could regress silently. START: add the missing client gate + negative tests (mirror saved.test.tsx) for vocabulary/book/topic writes, and cover the three progress/index data-isolation gates.

Name: S2 subject hub + assessment test coverage gaps (canStudy gate, assessment guard + result UI, /ready CTA)
Description: WHY: The S2 hub's masked-render path is only tested in isolation (canStudy is hardcoded true, so no producer ever masks a proxy viewer), and the assessment surface has no ownership guard test and no test of its pass/fail result UI. RISK: a proxy viewer sees full study/note affordances on the real hub (server blocks the write, but UX/regression is unguarded); the core assessment result screen has no protection. START: add a producer for canStudy + a masked-render integration test; add assessment guard + terminal-result-UI tests.

Name: Learning session resilience coverage gaps (crash-recovery hook + reflection-bonus display + TTS/challenge e2e)
Description: WHY: The streaming engine is gold-standard tested, but crash-recovery (use-session-recovery hook) has zero coverage and its e2e is parked, and the new V2 session-summary reflection-bonus display has zero assertions. RISK: kill-app-mid-session -> resume is unverified anywhere; the reflection 1.5x bonus XP could render wrong. START: add a use-session-recovery unit test (marker read/AppState/stale-marker) and assert reflectionBonusXp/bonusXpEarned on the summary screen (mirror the FirstSessionWrapUpCard test).

Name: Consent/profile gate coverage gaps (save-wizard adult gate + mentor-memory screen-wiring privacy writes)
Description: WHY: The save-wizard's minor-owner adult gate (defense-in-depth against a minor completing the wizard as owner) is untested at every layer, and the mentor-memory privacy writes are untested at the screen level. RISK: a regression to a legal/safety gate or a privacy-write control (consent grant, injection toggle, delete-all) would pass CI. START: add a minor-owner rejection test to ProfileBasicsStep + a wizard e2e; drive the consent-prompt/toggle/delete-all through the screen (hooks are already tested).

Name: Billing v2 live-path + child-facing + top-up test coverage gaps
Description: WHY: billing-v2 handlers are LIVE in prod (identity-v2 flag gone), yet the live quota hot path and family-seat billing have no real-DB tests, and the child-facing + top-up surfaces are largely uncovered. RISK: untested live billing code (quota decrement, family seat add/remove, top-up confirmation) can misbill or mis-provision with no safety net. START: add integration twins for quota-provision-v2 and family-v2, an e2e for the child in-chat quota card, and a top-up success->poll test + e2e.

Name: S5 visibility HTTP route-boundary test missing (routes/visibility.ts 6 endpoints)
Description: WHY: S5 safety invariants are proven at the service layer (real break-tests exist), but routes/visibility.ts (6 endpoints) has no HTTP-boundary test. RISK: route-layer wiring bugs (wrong caller-identity var, validator misconfig, dropped check before the service call) would ship despite green service tests. START: add Hono route tests asserting the caller-identity guards, zValidator enforcement, and safeSend/Inngest dispatch; scope around WI-1201 which covers the shared-record endpoint.

Name: S4 /now supporter artifact-exclusion negative test missing
Description: WHY: The supporter artifact-wall on /now (the highest-traffic supporter read surface) drops parked_item/ledger_moment for supporters, but has no negative-path test — unlike supporter-structural-mask.ts which is well covered. RISK: a regression could leak child free-text/transcript-adjacent content to a supporter feed undetected. START: seed a real supportership edge + child ledger/parked data and assert /now?scope=person and ?scope=supporter-hub exclude it, plus a no-edge -> 403 case.

Name: S0 /now route test coverage gaps (/now/overflow + 4 of 6 candidate kinds + person/supporter scopes)
Description: WHY: The /now ranker unit tests are strong, but the /now/overflow route has no test at any level and 4 of 6 candidate kinds + person/supporter scopes have no DB-level coverage. RISK: an untested route can 500 or return the wrong shape; DB query/join wiring for the untested kinds could silently return nothing. START: add a route test for /now/overflow and integration cases seeding each remaining candidate kind + aging-window promotion.

Name: Retire or fix stale + placeholder e2e yamls reporting false coverage
Description: WHY: Several Maestro yamls assert against removed copy/testIDs or dead UI states, so they pass (or will crash) while proving nothing — false coverage. RISK: green e2e that manufactures confidence; one (post-auth-comprehensive) will hard-crash on removed testIDs. START: for each listed yaml, either update assertions to current rendered behavior or delete the flow with a reason (never weaken to a stay-visible check).
